label: Flight Recorder: Home Assistant
info:
Flight recorder incidents delivered into Home Assistant: a persistent notification per incident, plus an incident counter you can dashboard and automate on.

REQUIREMENTS
This example uses the node-red-contrib-home-assistant-websocket palette (installed by default with the Home Assistant Node-RED add-on). Unlike the other examples, it needs two setup steps after import:

1. Open each of the two teal 'call service' nodes and select your Home Assistant server in the Server dropdown (on the add-on there is usually one preconfigured entry named 'Home Assistant').
2. In Home Assistant, create a Counter helper with entity id 'counter.flight_recorder_incidents' (Settings → Devices & services → Helpers → Create helper → Counter). Skip this step if you don't want the counter — the notification path works without it.

WHAT IT DOES
The top row is a plain flow whose worker errors every 5th message. The tap records the whole tab and auto-dumps on error. Each incident becomes:
- a persistent notification in HA (title, summary line, and where to find the full report), and
- one increment of the incident counter — dashboard it, or automate on it (e.g. notify your phone when it exceeds N in an hour).

ADAPTING IT
To notify your phone instead, change the notification 'call service' node's domain/service to notify / mobile_app_<your_device> and adjust the data built by the 'build notification' function (mobile notifications use message/title at the top level rather than under data). To archive full reports alongside, set the report node's destination to 'message + file' with a directory such as /share/flight-recorder/reports — the notification already tells you where to look.

The incident cooldown is set to 60s so a repeating fault can't flood your notification panel.

name: A plain flow — the worker errors every 5th message
worker (errors every 5th)
Tap auto-dumps on error → each incident becomes an HA notification + counter tick
